summary: Game-cheat package with Microsoft-impersonating persistence, eval of obfuscated payload, host-ID exfiltration, and destructive auto-update
details: sensivity@2.5.46 is a Windows-targeted package whose load and CLI paths perform multiple hostile actions against installers. launcher.js, executed at module load via the worker branch, ends with `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js','utf8'))`, evaluating a 142 KB heavily obfuscated blob (obfuscator.io output: 692-entry rotated string array, RC4 string decoder, control-flow flattening) with no integrity check. Once decoded, server.obf.js reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell, the C: volume serial via wmic, and os.hostname(), SHA-256s the concatenation, and POSTs the digest plus app/version/nonce to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL hidden inside the obfuscation. launcher.js installs reboot persistence by writing HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\OneDriveUpdate to launch a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that runs `node.exe launcher.js` with a hidden window — impersonating a Microsoft OneDrive component — and sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to masquerade as a Windows OS process. cli.js subcommand `update` issues `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe`, killing every Node and PowerShell process on the host (destructive collateral on developer/CI machines), then runs `npm i -g sensivity@latest`, giving the author a mutable channel to silently push future payloads. The package additionally ships sens.node, a 6.5 MB native module whose strings ('FiveM', 'GTAProcess', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot/LeftHand/Head', '__licenseAccepted') identify it as a FiveM/GTA V aimbot/ESP cheat dispatched by server.obf.js (forte.start/stop/setConfig/getStatus). Multiple independent attack fingerprints stack: hidden-payload eval at load time, hardcoded outbound POST of host identifiers, Run-key persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name, process-name camouflage, indiscriminate process termination, and unpinned mutable self-update.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
